items.desc: Russia's annual Victory Day parade will be smaller than previous ones as the effects of the war on Ukraine take a toll.

items.title: Some people in Iran have kept internet access despite government-imposed blackout
items.desc: Iran is experiencing the longest internet blackout ever recorded with 99% of the population offline. But certain people  — with "white internet" — have stayed connected this entire time.

items.desc: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Rep. Bill Keating of Massachusetts about continued U.S. military strikes on suspected drug boats in Pacific and Caribbean waters.

items.title: Trump's Truth Social lays bare narrow obsessions of an extremely online president

items.title: Ukraine Ramps Up Drone Strikes Ahead of Moscow’s Parade
items.desc: Ukraine conducted heavy overnight drone attacks on Russia, giving no indication that Kyiv is ready to observe Moscow’s self-declared ceasefire despite threats of retaliation if Victory Day celebrations on Red Square are disrupted.

items.title: Nintendo to hike Switch 2 price, warns on profits
items.desc: Japanese gaming giant Nintendo said today it will hike the price of its Switch 2 gaming console as memory chip costs soar, warning that net profit would fall 27% this year.
